old: 曾祖父是爷爷的父亲。
new: 他的曾祖父活到了九十多岁。

old: Great-grandfather is grandfather's father.
new: His great-grandfather lived past ninety.

old: 曾孙是孙子的孩子。
new: 他终于见到了自己的曾孙。

old: A great-grandchild is the child of one's grandchild.
new: He finally got to meet his great-grandchild.

old: 用电的基本常识很重要。
new: 开车前要了解基本的交通常识。
reason: Near-duplicate of sentence_1: both sentences use electricity safety as the illustrating scenario.

old: Basic common sense about using electricity is very important.
new: It is important to understand basic traffic common sense before driving.

old: The doctor asked him to rest thoroughly for a week.
new: The doctor asked him to get a complete rest for a week.
reason: Awkward English: 'rest thoroughly' is unnatural; 'complete rest' is the idiomatic phrasing.

old: 他用秤称了称米的重量。
new: 他用秤称了称米。
reason: Redundant: 称了称 already means 'weighed (a bit)'; appending 重量 is unnecessary and makes the sentence awkward.

old: The composition of this alloy is very complex.
new: The components of this alloy are very complex.
reason: Translation uses 'composition' but the definition being illustrated is 'component'; 'components' aligns with the target definition.

old: 经过多年，他变得更成熟。
new: 这家公司的管理制度已经很成熟了。
reason: Near-duplicate of sentence_1: both describe a male subject gradually becoming more mature over time.

old: After many years, he became more mature.
new: This company's management system is already very mature.
reason: Matches corrected sentence_3.

old: 她一个人承受了所有的痛苦。
new: 这根柱子能承受建筑的重量。
reason: Cross-row near-duplicate with row 3120 sentence_1 (他承受了失去父亲的痛苦): both feature bearing pain or suffering.

old: She bore all the pain alone.
new: This pillar can bear the weight of the building.
reason: Matches corrected sentence_2.

old: verb
new: adjective
reason: 冲 meaning 'blunt/brusque' functions as a predicate adjective in all three sentences (说话很冲, 态度太冲), not as a verb.

old: He made abundant use of his time.
new: He made full use of his time.
reason: 充分利用 collocates with 'full use' or 'thorough use' in English; 'abundant use' is unnatural for this fixed phrase.

old: This sweater shrunk a little after washing.
new: This sweater shrank a little after washing.
reason: 'Shrunk' is the past participle; the simple past tense of 'shrink' is 'shrank'.

old: His trousers shrunk and he can no longer put them on.
new: His trousers shrank and he can no longer put them on.
reason: 'Shrunk' is the past participle; the simple past tense of 'shrink' is 'shrank'.

old: 他的工作表现十分出色。

old: His work performance is very outstanding.

old: This is just our initial plan.
new: When he first arrived here, he was not familiar with anything.

old: 最初的结果很好，大家很高兴。
new: 这项工程初步完成，大家都很高兴。

old: The initial results are good; everyone is pleased.
new: The project has been initially completed; everyone is very pleased.
reason: Matches corrected sentence_3.

old: 这件事不好处，让人头疼。

old: This matter is not easy to handle; it gives people a headache.
